title: Glossary
content: Glossary '> Systems 74 Directories 24 Components 55 Articles 5 Blueprints 7 Guides 18 Glossary 46 0 1 2 Biweekly Digest Request Ad [./advertisement] Created by Ilya Greben [https://ilyagreben.com] Accessibility Atomic Design Atom Breakpoints Color system Component Component library Data visualization Design language Design System Design System Principles Design token Elevation Figma Framework Grid System Icons Information architecture Layout Material Design Molecule Motion Organism Pattern Prototype Properties Repository Responsive Single Source of Truth Slots Spacing States Storybook Style Guide System architecture Theming Typography UI Kit Usability User-centered design User Experience (UX) User Interface (UI) Visual hierarchy Voice and Tone Wireframes 0 1 2 Biweekly Digest Keep up with the latest in Design Systems Subscribe Ad 0 1 2 Biweekly Digest Keep up with the latest in Design Systems Subscribe Ad # Glossary Glossary # Glossary Glossary 01 ## Accessibility Available in the Database 49 sources [./directories/accessibility] Accessibility ensures designs are usable by people with disabilities, adhering to guidelines such as WCAG. It involves considering color contrast, screen readers, keyboard navigation, and more to create inclusive digital experiences for all users. Database Database Guides Guides Advertisement 02 ## Atomic Design Atomic Design is a methodology for creating Design Systems [./guides/design-system], organizing components [./guides/component] hierarchically. It begins with the smallest elements (atoms) and builds up to molecules, organisms, templates, and pages, promoting scalability and consistency. Guides Guides 03 ## Atom Atom in Atomic Design [./guides/atomic-design] refers to the smallest, indivisible elements of a design—like buttons [./components/button], labels [./components/label], or text fields [./components/text-field]. These are foundational components [./components] that, when combined, form more complex UI elements. 04 ## Breakpoints Breakpoints define specific screen widths at which a design layout [./guides/layout] changes to provide a responsive experience. These are essential in responsive web design to ensure usability [./guides/usability] across devices like desktops, tablets, and smartphones. 05 ## Color system Available in the Database 70 sources [./directories/color-system] Color system organizes a palette into semantically understandable and reusable values, with variations for different states and contexts. It ensures consistency across a UI and helps in achieving brand alignment and accessibility [./guides/accessibility] standards. Database Database Guides Guides 06 ## Component Available in the Database 55 categories [./components] Component is a reusable, self-contained UI element, like a button [./components/button] or card [./components/cards]. It encapsulates HTML, CSS, and sometimes JavaScript functionality, enabling designers and developers to build modular and scalable interfaces. Database Database Guides Guides 07 ## Component library Component library is a collection of pre-built, reusable UI components [./guides/component], each styled consistently according to a Design System [./guides/design-system]. It streamlines the design and development process by promoting reuse and reducing design inconsistencies. 08 ## Data visualization Available in the Database 23 categories [./directories/data-visualization] Data visualization transforms complex data sets into visual formats like charts, graphs, and maps. It enables users to understand trends, patterns, and insights at a glance, aiding in decision-making and storytelling. 09 ## Design language Design language is a unified set of design principles, guidelines, and visual elements that ensure consistency across products. It encompasses typography [./guides/typography], colors [./guides/color], icons [./guides/icons], and patterns [./guides/pattern], shaping the overall user experience [./guides/user-experience]. Advertisement 10 ## Design System Available in the Database 74 sources [./design-systems] Design System is a set of standards, components [./guides/component], and guidelines that provide a consistent design language across digital products. It promotes efficiency, scalability, and cohesion in design and development workflows. Database Database Guides Guides 11 ## Design System principles Design System principles are foundational guidelines that shape the design and development of a system. They focus on consistency, scalability, usability [./guides/usability], and accessibility [./guides/accessibility], ensuring a unified user experience [./guides/user-experience] across platforms. 12 ## Design token Available in the Database 37 sources [./directories/tokens] Design tokens are variables used to store design values such as colors [./guides/color], typography [./guides/typography], spacing [./guides/spacing] and other values. They ensure consistency across platforms by providing a Single Source of Truth [./guides/single-source-of-truth] for design-related properties in a system. Database Database Guides Guides 13 ## Elevation Available in the Database 32 sources [./directories/elevation] Elevation refers to the visual layering of UI elements through shadows or z-index, creating depth and hierarchy in design. It's commonly used in Material Design [./design-systems/google], Gitlab Pajamas [./design-systems/gitlab], Atlassian Design System [./design-systems/atlassian], Microsoft Fluent 2 [./design-systems/microsoft], and other Design Systems [./guides/design-system] to indicate the importance and interactivity of elements. Database Database Guides Guides 14 ## Figma Available in the Database 31 sources [./directories/figma] Figma is a web-based design and prototyping tool that allows teams to collaborate in real time. It supports the creation of UI/UX designs, prototyping, and Design System [./guides/design-system] management, enhancing workflow efficiency. Database Database 15 ## Framework Framework is a pre-built structure of code and components that developers use to build applications. Popular front-end frameworks like React, Vue, and Angular simplify the development process by providing reusable solutions. 16 ## Grid system Grid system is a layout framework that helps align and organize content on a page. It creates visual structure and balance, ensuring responsive, consistent designs across different devices and screen sizes. 17 ## Icons Available in the Database 62 sources [./directories/icons] Icons are small, graphic symbols used to represent actions, objects, or ideas. They improve user understanding by visually conveying meaning, often simplifying text-heavy interfaces and enhancing navigation. Database Database Guides Guides 18 ## Information architecture Information architecture refers to the organization and structure of information within a product. It helps users find and navigate content efficiently, improving overall usability [./guides/usability] and user experience [./guides/user-experience]. Advertisement 19 ## Layout Available in the Database 45 sources [./directories/layout] Layout refers to the arrangement of elements on a page or screen. It ensures visual hierarchy, balance, and usability [./guides/usability] by organizing components [./guides/component] in a way that supports user goals and aligns with the design's purpose. Database Database Guides Guides 20 ## Material Design Material Design is a design system created by Google [./design-systems/google] that emphasizes consistency across devices. It uses principles like grid-based layouts [./guides/layout], responsive animations, and depth effects [./guides/elevation] like shadows to create intuitive user interfaces [./guides/user-interface]. 21 ## Molecule In Atomic Design [./guides/atomic-design], a molecule is a group of atoms combined to form a more complex UI element, such as a search form [./components/form] (text field [./components/text-field] and button [./components/button]). Molecules are the building blocks for more intricate components [./guides/component] and organisms. 22 ## Motion Available in the Database 30 sources [./directories/motion] Motion in design refers to the use of animation and transitions to create fluid interactions. It guides users through an interface, adds context, and enhances user experience [./guides/user-experience] by making digital environments feel more responsive. Database Database 23 ## Organism In Atomic Design [./guides/atomic-design], an organism is a relatively complex group of molecules that function together as a unit, like a navigation bar. It is one of the higher levels in the design hierarchy, built from smaller atomic units. 24 ## Pattern Available in the Database 34 sources [./directories/patterns] Pattern is a repeatable design solution to common user interface [./guides/user-interface] problems. It provides best practices and consistency across a product, improving usability [./guides/usability] and reducing cognitive load for users. Database Database Guides Guides 25 ## Prototype Prototype is interactive, low- to high-fidelity representations of a product. They simulate the user experience [./guides/user-experience], allowing designers and stakeholders to test and refine interactions before full development begins. 26 ## Properties Properties in design refer to attributes assigned to elements, such as color [./guides/color], size, or typography [./guides/typography]. They are the building blocks of style, helping define how components [./guides/component] look and behave across various states [./directories/states]. 27 ## Repository Available in the Database 49 sources [./directories/repository] Repository is a storage location where code, design assets, or documentation are stored and managed. In Design Systems [./guides/design-system], repositories ensure that all components [./guides/component], guidelines, and tokens [./guides/design-token] are accessible and maintained consistently. Database Database Advertisement 28 ## Responsive Available in the Database 20 sources [./directories/responsive] Responsive design adapts layouts to various screen sizes and devices, providing an optimal user experience [./guides/user-experience] on mobile, tablet, and desktop. It uses breakpoints, fluid grids, and flexible images to ensure content adjusts dynamically. Database Database 29 ## Single Source of Truth Single Source of Truth refers to having a centralized reference point for design or development assets. In a Design System [./guides/design-system], this ensures that all teams use consistent components [./guides/component], tokens [./guides/design-token], and guidelines, reducing inconsistencies. Guides Guides 31 ## Slots Slots refer to placeholder areas within components [./guides/component] that allow for content to be dynamically inserted. They offer flexibility, enabling the customization of reusable components without altering their core structure or functionality. 32 ## Spacing Available in the Database 46 sources [./directories/spacing] Spacing refers to the use of padding, margins, and gaps between UI elements. Proper spacing ensures clarity, improves readability, and enhances the overall aesthetic and usability [./guides/usability] of a design, contributing to a balanced layout [./guides/layout]. Database Database Guides Guides 33 ## States Available in the Database 7 sources [./directories/states] States are variations of UI components [./guides/component] that indicate their behavior in different scenarios, such as hover, active, or disabled states. They provide users with visual feedback, improving interactivity and the overall user experience [./guides/user-experience]. Database Database 34 ## Storybook Available in the Database 23 sources [./directories/storybook] Storybook is an open-source tool for developing UI components [./guides/component] in isolation. It allows designers and developers to create, view, and test individual components outside the application, ensuring reusability and consistency. Database Database 35 ## Style guide Style guide is a comprehensive document that outlines the rules for typography [./guides/typography], color [./guides/color], icons [./guides/icons], and other design elements. It ensures consistency across all user interfaces [./guides/user-interface] and reflects the brand’s visual identity. 36 ## System architecture System architecture in design refers to the overall structure of a Design System [./guides/design-system], including its components [./guides/component], tokens [./guides/design-token], and documentation. It provides a blueprint for how the system scales and integrates across teams and platforms. Advertisement 37 ## Theming Available in the Database 23 sources [./directories/theming] Theming refers to the application of specific styles, such as colors [./guides/color] and typography [./guides/typography], to alter the appearance of a UI [./guides/user-interface] while keeping the underlying structure intact. It allows for customization without changing the core components [./guides/component]. Database Database 38 ## Typography Available in the Database 69 sources [./directories/typography] Typography refers to the style, arrangement, and appearance of text in a design. It plays a critical role in readability, visual hierarchy, and user experience [./guides/user-experience], impacting how users perceive and interact with content. Database Database Guides Guides 39 ## UI Kit UI Kit is a collection of pre-designed UI elements, like buttons [./components/button], text fields [./components/text-field], and icons [./components/icon], that are reusable across projects. It provides a set of standardized components [./guides/component], enabling faster design and development while maintaining consistency. Guides Guides 40 ## Usability Usability measures how easily and efficiently users can interact with a product or system. It focuses on ease of use, user satisfaction, and error reduction, ensuring that users can achieve their goals with minimal friction. Guides Guides 41 ## User-centered design User-centered design is a design approach that prioritizes the needs, behaviors, and goals of the user. It involves user research, prototyping, and testing to create intuitive, user-friendly products that solve real problems. 42 ## User Experience (UX) User experience (UX) encompasses all aspects of a user's interaction with a product, focusing on usability [./guides/usability], functionality, and emotional satisfaction. A well-designed UX ensures users find value, efficiency, and enjoyment in the product. Guides Guides 43 ## User Interface (UI) User interface (UI) refers to the visual elements through which users interact with a product. It includes components like buttons [./components/button], forms [./components/form], and menus [./components/menus], focusing on aesthetics, usability [./guides/usability], and user interaction. Guides Guides 44 ## Visual hierarchy Visual hierarchy organizes elements in a design by importance, guiding users' attention and helping them process information effectively. Through size, color [./guides/color], and spacing [./guides/spacing], designers prioritize key content and improve usability [./guides/usability]. 45 ## Voice and Tone Available in the Database 40 sources [./directories/voice-and-tone] Voice and tone refers to the consistent personality of a brand, while tone changes based on context and audience. Together, they shape how users perceive the brand, influencing emotional engagement and the overall user experience [./guides/user-experience]. Database Database 46 ## Wireframes Wireframes are low-fidelity, simplified visual representations of a design's structure. They outline key elements like navigation, content layout, and functionality, serving as the foundation for the final visual design. Advertisement ## What is a Design System Glossary? Glossary explains key Design System concepts and terms with clear descriptions, providing links to directories and components database, along with practical guidelines to enhance understanding and usage Design Systems [./design-systems] Apple HIG [./design-systems/apple] Google Material Design [./design-systems/google] IBM Design System [./design-systems/ibm] Microsoft Fluent 2 [./design-systems/microsoft] Directories [./directories] Accessibility [./directories/accessibility] Color System [./directories/color-system] Figma [./directories/figma] Typography [./directories/typography] Components [./components] Button [./components/button] Checkbox [./components/checkbox] Radio button [./components/radio-button] Text field [./components/text-field] Design Systems .surf  Share feedback [https://forms.gle/ADApdgVpd6XBcCYM8] Created by Ilya Greben [https://ilyagreben.com] '>

A well-designed UX ensures users find value, efficiency, and enjoyment in the product. Guides Guides 43 ## User Interface (UI) User interface (UI) refers to the visual elements through which users interact with a product. It includes components like buttons [./components/button], forms [./components/form], and menus [./components/menus], focusing on aesthetics, usability [./guides/usability], and user interaction. Guides Guides 44 ## Visual hierarchy Visual hierarchy organizes elements in a design by importance, guiding users' attention and helping them process information effectively. esign. Advertisement ## What is a Design System Glossary?
